Sro<e ol€olifornio
Memorondum
To : Mssrs. Jack Gordon and Noel Robinson, 
Dote : September 27, 1976
Representatives of the Franchise
Tax Board before the Fair PoliticaL
Practices Commission on the Review of the
Conffict of Interest Code of the
Franchise Tax Board
from , FA|R poLtTtcAL pRAcTtcEs coMMtsstoN - Delbert L' Spurlock. Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division
Subiecr: Conflict of Interest Code of the Franchise Tax Board
The proposed Conflict of Interest Code of the Franchise Tax
Board has been revised and approved by the Fair Political Practices
Commission pursuant to Government Code Section 87303 (b) . 
The
Commission revisions to the proposed Code are in conformity with
the proposed staff amendments contained in a memorandum from
Delbert Spur]ock to Comnission memlrers of the Fair Political
Practices Commission dated SePtember 15, 1976 (amending previous
memoranda on the same subject) except that, in addition, the
Commission deleted from the ncategory If" of desj-gnated positions
on Page 3 of the proposed Code the Positions of:
(1) "Tax Auditor I, r'T8", and
(2) "Tax Representative Trainee".
The complete revised Code as apProved by the Commission is
attached.
I thank both of you, other members of the staff, and the
Board for undertaking the arduous task of "being first" 
and
helping my staff in resolving many of the difficult 
issues which
faced us at the beginning of this process.
If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me
at 322-6444.
DLS:ma
Attachment
/h- F6 ' 567


irote ol colifornio
'Memorqndum
To : 
I\tr. Charles C. Haskins
Chief Deputy State Treasurer
Dote , September 28, 1976
From : FAtR pOLtTtCAL PRACTICES COI\4M|SS|ON - Edgar A. Kerry, Sta f f Attorney
Conf l-icts of Interest Division
sub;ecr: Approval, as Revised, of Conflict of Interest Code of the
Office of the State Treasurer
f. 
The proposed Conflict of Interest Code for the state
Treasurer's Office has been revised and approved, as revised, by
the Fair Politica1 Practices Commission pursuant to Government
Code S87303(b) . The Commission's revisions are in conformity
with proposed staff amendments contained in the memoranda dated
September 14 and 22, L975, from Ed Kerry to the members of the
Fair Pol-itical Practices Commission. fn addition, the Commission
made the following revisions:
A. fnserted the words "including l"oans" after the
word "income" in each subparagraph of Section VI (see revi-
sion (12), attached) ; and
B. Inserted the words "with any water, irrigation,
sewage or other district 
whose issuance of bonds or other
securities is subject to the approval of", at page 5, Section
VIf A Designated Employee j-n croup C, line 6 (see revision
(12 ) , attached) .
II. 
A copy of your revised and approved Code, with attached
revisions, is enclosed, Unfortunately, due to staff limitations
r^re are unable to retype your Code with the revisions i-ncorporated.
This is left to your own agency to compl-ete.
III. 
Pursuant to Government Code S\7422, your Code will
become effective on the 30th day after its date of filing with the
Secretary of State. To coordinate the date for filing initial 
state-
ments of economj-c interests under your Code, with the date selected
(January 31st) for filing annual statements thereafter, you might
wish to file your Code with the Secretary of State on or about
Decernber 2, 1976. Your Code will- then be effective January 7, 1977i
your "designated employees" will file initial 
statements within
30 days (by January 3L, 1977), and annual statements will be filed
each January 31 thereafter.


Charles C. Haskins
September 28, 197 6
Page Two
If you file your Code with the Secretary of State prior
to the above recommended date (December 2, 1976), your "designated
employees" will have a doubfe filing requirement within a very
short time period. They will be required to file initial 
state-
ments within 30 days after the effective date of your Code, and
they will be required to file annual statements shortly thereafter,
on January 31 , L977. To avoid this unnecessary burden on your
"designated employees", the filing of your Code with the Secretary
of State on or about December 2, 1977, is recommended.
IV. If I can be of further help to you, please feel free to
contact me at 322-6444.
EAK : mra
At tachmen ts

sections.question_synthetic: This document does not contain a traditional legal question but rather communicates FPPC approval of revised Conflict of Interest Codes for two state agencies (Franchise Tax Board and State Treasurer's Office) with specified modifications.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has approved the revised Conflict of Interest Codes for both the Franchise Tax Board and State Treasurer's Office, with specific deletions and insertions made to designated positions and income disclosure requirements. The State Treasurer's Code is recommended for filing with the Secretary of State on or about December 2, 1976, to coordinate the effective date with annual statement filing deadlines.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate memoranda (one to Franchise Tax Board representatives dated 9/27/76, one to State Treasurer dated 9/28/76). No traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; structured as approval notices with specific code revisions. Document ID 76569 referenced on first memo. Confidence moderate due to lack of standard Q&A format and OCR artifacts in source text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: This document does not contain a traditional legal question but rather communicates FPPC approval of revised Conflict of Interest Codes for two state agencies (Franchise Tax Board and State Treasurer's Office) with specified modifications.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has approved the revised Conflict of Interest Codes for both the Franchise Tax Board and State Treasurer's Office, with specific deletions and insertions made to designated positions and income disclosure requirements. The State Treasurer's Code is recommended for filing with the Secretary of State on or about December 2, 1976, to coordinate the effective date with annual statement filing deadlines.

embedding.summary: This memorandum package contains two FPPC approval letters from September 1976 notifying state agencies (Franchise Tax Board and State Treasurer's Office) that their revised Conflict of Interest Codes have been approved by the Commission with specified revisions to designated positions and disclosure requirements.
